regimeSub: Risk-on relief as US-Iran deal cracks the oil premium, but sticky 4.2% CPI keeps Warsh's Fed pinned on hold.
themes: US-Iran peace deal collapses war risk premium -> Brent to 3-month lows, equities to record highs
Energy-led CPI spike to 4.2% meets falling oil -> inflation impulse peaks, but Fed won't pre-cut
Warsh arrives dovish-by-reputation but boxed by hot prints -> hold now, slow normalization later
Falling oil + AI/IPO euphoria -> record SPX, but breadth concentrated in semis and mega-cap

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: ~-0.6% real on 10y vs 4.2% CPI; still loose, but disinflation from oil should turn reals less negative.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +39bp, modestly steep; market pricing growth resilience and a patient Fed, not recession.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 16 and record equities signal no credit stress, full risk appetite post-deal.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.2 and falling — war-premium unwind complete; complacency risk if Iran deal frays.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $83 off the highs on Iran deal; downside capped by lingering Mideast risk premium per CNBC.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4336, holding near highs despite de-escalation — debasement/fiscal hedge bid persists.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1512 weak-KRW; DXY 99.7 soft, but KRW lagging — watch BOK and semi export cycle.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Nvidia/Micron/Sandisk leading the melt-up; AI capex and IPO wave driving record tape.

headline: A US-Iran peace deal cracked oil and propelled global equities to records, even as a 3-year-high 4.2% CPI leaves Warsh's Fed on hold and gold pinned near all-time highs.
